atomic.c(3) Library Functions Manual atomic.c(3)

NAME

atomic.c - Debug formatting and state tracking for atomic operations.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void debug_atomic_init (void)
Lifecycle initialization for debug atomic tracking. void debug_atomic_shutdown (void)
Lifecycle shutdown for debug atomic tracking. bool debug_atomic_is_initialized (void)
Check if debug atomic tracking is initialized. int debug_atomic_format_timing (const atomic_t *atomic, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format timing info for an atomic and write to buffer. int debug_atomic_ptr_format_timing (const atomic_ptr_t *atomic, char *buffer, size_t size)
Format timing info for an atomic_ptr_t and write to buffer. void debug_atomic_print_state (void)
Print all named atomic operations state (integration with --sync-state).

Detailed Description

Debug formatting and state tracking for atomic operations.

Date

March 2026

Implements debug-specific functions: initialization/shutdown, and state formatting for display via --sync-state.

Hook implementations (atomic_on_load, atomic_on_store, etc.) are now in lib/atomic.c alongside the wrapper functions they support.

Definition in file debug/atomic.c.

Function Documentation

int debug_atomic_format_timing (const atomic_t * atomic, char * buffer, size_t size)

Format timing info for an atomic and write to buffer. Format timing info for an atomic_t to a buffer.

Parameters

atomic Pointer to atomic_t
buffer Output buffer
size Buffer size

Returns

Number of bytes written to buffer

Definition at line 53 of file debug/atomic.c.

53                                                                                   {
54     if (!atomic || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
55 
56     // If never accessed, return empty (so caller can skip output)
57     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns == 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns == 0) {
58         return 0;
59     }
60 
61     int offset = 0;
62     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
63 
64     char load_str[64] = "";
65     char store_str[64] = "";
66     char count_str[128] = "";
67 
68     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_load_time_ns <= now_ns) {
69         char elapsed_str[64];
70         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_load_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
71         snprintf(load_str, sizeof(load_str), "load=%s", elapsed_str);
72     }
73 
74     if (atomic->last_store_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns <= now_ns) {
75         char elapsed_str[64];
76         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_store_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
77         snprintf(store_str, sizeof(store_str), "store=%s", elapsed_str);
78     }
79 
80     if (atomic->load_count > 0 || atomic->store_count > 0 || atomic->cas_count > 0 || atomic->fetch_count > 0) {
81         snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: load=%llu store=%llu cas=%llu/%llu fetch=%llu]",
82                  (unsigned long long)atomic->load_count, (unsigned long long)atomic->store_count,
83                  (unsigned long long)atomic->cas_success_count, (unsigned long long)atomic->cas_count,
84                  (unsigned long long)atomic->fetch_count);
85     }
86 
87     offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s", load_str, store_str, count_str);
88     return offset;
89 }




References atomic_t::cas_count,
    atomic_t::cas_success_count, atomic_t::fetch_count,
    atomic_t::last_load_time_ns, atomic_t::last_store_time_ns,
    atomic_t::load_count, atomic_t::store_count,
    time_get_ns(), and time_pretty().






void
  debug_atomic_init (void )


Lifecycle initialization for debug atomic tracking. In release
    builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op macro. In debug builds, this initializes
    atomic operation tracking.


Definition at line 30 of file debug/atomic.c.


30                              {
31     g_atomic_debug_initialized = true;
32 }






Referenced by main().






bool debug_atomic_is_initialized (void )


Check if debug atomic tracking is initialized. In release builds
    (NDEBUG), returns true. In debug builds, returns actual initialization
    state.


Definition at line 38 of file debug/atomic.c.


38                                        {
39     return g_atomic_debug_initialized;
40 }






Referenced by atomic_on_cas(), atomic_on_fetch(),
    atomic_on_load(), atomic_on_store(),
    atomic_ptr_on_cas(), atomic_ptr_on_exchange(),
    atomic_ptr_on_load(), and atomic_ptr_on_store().






void
  debug_atomic_print_state (void )


Print all named atomic operations state (integration with
    --sync-state). Called by debug_sync_print_state() to include atomic
    operations in sync state output. In release builds (NDEBUG), this is a no-op
    macro. In debug builds, this prints detailed atomic operation
  statistics.


Definition at line 136 of file debug/atomic.c.


136                                     {
137     if (!g_atomic_debug_initialized) return;
138 
139     // Iterate named registry for atomic entries
140     // Note: this is now kept for backward compatibility
141     // The preferred method is to use sync.c's atomic_iter_callback
142     // which integrates atomics into the synchronized output buffer
143 }










int
  debug_atomic_ptr_format_timing (const atomic_ptr_t * atomic, char *
  buffer, size_t size)


Format timing info for an atomic_ptr_t and write to buffer.
    Format timing info for an atomic_ptr_t to a buffer.




Parameters


atomic Pointer to atomic_ptr_t


buffer Output buffer


size Buffer size


Returns


Number of bytes written to buffer


Definition at line 98 of file debug/atomic.c.


98                                                                                           {
99     if (!atomic || !buffer || size == 0) return 0;
100 
101     // If never accessed, return empty (so caller can skip output)
102     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns == 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns == 0) {
103         return 0;
104     }
105 
106     int offset = 0;
107     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
108 
109     char load_str[64] = "";
110     char store_str[64] = "";
111     char count_str[128] = "";
112 
113     if (atomic->last_load_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_load_time_ns <= now_ns) {
114         char elapsed_str[64];
115         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_load_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
116         snprintf(load_str, sizeof(load_str), "load=%s", elapsed_str);
117     }
118 
119     if (atomic->last_store_time_ns > 0 && atomic->last_store_time_ns <= now_ns) {
120         char elapsed_str[64];
121         time_pretty(now_ns - atomic->last_store_time_ns, -1, elapsed_str, sizeof(elapsed_str));
122         snprintf(store_str, sizeof(store_str), "store=%s", elapsed_str);
123     }
124 
125     if (atomic->load_count > 0 || atomic->store_count > 0 || atomic->cas_count > 0 || atomic->exchange_count > 0) {
126         snprintf(count_str, sizeof(count_str), "[ops: load=%llu store=%llu cas=%llu/%llu exchange=%llu]",
127                  (unsigned long long)atomic->load_count, (unsigned long long)atomic->store_count,
128                  (unsigned long long)atomic->cas_success_count, (unsigned long long)atomic->cas_count,
129                  (unsigned long long)atomic->exchange_count);
130     }
131 
132     offset += snprintf(buffer + offset, size - offset, "%s %s %s", load_str, store_str, count_str);
133     return offset;
134 }






References atomic_ptr_t::cas_count,
    atomic_ptr_t::cas_success_count, atomic_ptr_t::exchange_count,
    atomic_ptr_t::last_load_time_ns,
    atomic_ptr_t::last_store_time_ns, atomic_ptr_t::load_count,
    atomic_ptr_t::store_count, time_get_ns(), and
    time_pretty().






void
  debug_atomic_shutdown (void )


Lifecycle shutdown for debug atomic tracking. In release builds
    (NDEBUG), this is a no-op macro. In debug builds, this cleans up atomic
    operation tracking.


Definition at line 34 of file debug/atomic.c.


34                                  {
35     g_atomic_debug_initialized = false;
36 }






Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().
